title: After 3I why does Asuka regain life before the others
display_title: After 3I why does Asuka regain life before the others
wikitext:
[]: After 3I, why does Asuka regain life before the others?             Quote Post by Kane of Nod » Fri Apr 13, 2012 7:25 pm I searched for threads related to this, but I haven't found an answer to my question. I don't understand why Asuka comes back to life before everyone else. Some people, from what I've seen, think that it was Shinji's decision, but that doesn't make sense to me because it isn't implied, let alone stated, that he has that power at any point in EoE. I understand that only those with the will to live will come back, just like Yui said. What I don't understand is why Asuka would come back before anyone else.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Fri Apr 13, 2012 7:47 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by Kane of Nod » Fri Apr 13, 2012 7:56 pm Seele00TextOnly wrote: Because that's how cool Asuka is. Besides didn't you hear her earlier in the movie? 'I don't want to DIEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEE' :P

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Fri Apr 13, 2012 8:06 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by Kane of Nod » Fri Apr 13, 2012 8:25 pm Maybe she was just thinking of Shinji while getting untanged. Anyways, I just assumed that people who at least were close to Shinji would gravitate towards his position, and that people travel at light speed while in the LCL, so position wouldn't be a problem. I need to rewatch the episode where Shinji gets tanged to get a better idea.

[]:             Quote Post by Henry Spencer » Fri Apr 13, 2012 10:18 pm I guess part of this could lie in Asuka's actions in the first half of the movie. The first thing we hear her say is "I don't want to die" over and over again, after all. Then, when she actually does (temporarily) die, it looks like she's doing everything in her power to fight death. Meanwhile, everyone in Instrumentality was either pissing themselves in panic or crying tears of joy as they explode into Hawaiian Punch. Or died minutes before it occurred. But still, there's a difference.

[]:             Quote Post by Fireball » Fri Apr 13, 2012 10:34 pm IdontwanttodieIdontwanttodieIdontwanttodieIdontwanttodieIdontwanttodieIdontwanttodieIdontwanttodieIdontwanttodie~ It's a testament to her willpower.

[]:             Quote Post by Korgan » Sat Apr 14, 2012 2:48 pm Maybe she was just thinking of Shinji while getting untanged. Anyways, I just assumed that people who at least were close to Shinji would gravitate towards his position, and that people travel at light speed while in the LCL, so position wouldn't be a problem.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Apr 14, 2012 2:56 pm Maybe she didn't. Maybe she was just the first to spawn next to where Shinji was. There's a whole damn planet for people to crawl out of the ocean, you guys. There might be a community of like 150 people over in New York or London by the time Asuka shows up.

[]:             Quote Post by Korgan » Sat Apr 14, 2012 3:00 pm What's *really* surprising to me, though, is Shinji's "memorial" where he hanged Misato's cross. He had been told that all of the recently killed can come back if they want to! Did he expect her to remain tanged? Or that her soul didn't get into the Instrumentality sphere because she died too early? (What happens to a soul after a normal death? Does it go to the 'Chamber of Guf' and dissolve there, or is there an afterlife for it?) Or was the memorial not to Misato? WTF?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Apr 14, 2012 4:00 pm He even had access to Misato's memories of her week with Kaji during the Complementation process. This leads weight to the "a lot of time has passed" view (and to the "the pilots are in their own special damnation" view too).

[]: Re: After 3I, why does Asuka regain life before the others?             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Apr 14, 2012 5:45 pm I think Rei got tired of watching him slide into oblivion, so she kicked Asuka out of the pool to give him some company.

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Sat Apr 14, 2012 7:01 pm Korgan wrote: What's *really* surprising to me, though, is Shinji's "memorial" where he hanged Misato's cross. He had been told that all of the recently killed can come back if they want to! Did he expect her to remain tanged? Or that her soul didn't get into the Instrumentality sphere because she died too early? (What happens to a soul after a normal death? Does it go to the 'Chamber of Guf' and dissolve there, or is there an afterlife for it?) Or was the memorial not to Misato? WTF?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Apr 14, 2012 7:35 pm Thing is, Instrumenality is rejected for everybody. It's just seperated souls (minus Shinji & Asuka) reduced to L.C.L.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Sat Apr 14, 2012 9:02 pm Jornophelanthas wrote: I believe Misato is one of the less likely people to return.

[]:             Quote Post by Kane of Nod » Sat Apr 14, 2012 10:19 pm Azathoth wrote: As to why Asuka's there before anyone else, it's because she, above anyone else, wanted not to be killed by Shinji.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Sun Apr 15, 2012 12:47 am Possible, of course, although the argument about what exactly is making her kimochi warui could last all day (and, in the past, has). But I'm more referring to her line in the script for that scene, in which she outright states that she would rather live than die knowing it was Shinji's fault.

[]: Re: After 3I, why does Asuka regain life before the others?             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Sun Apr 15, 2012 12:54 am NemZ wrote: I think Rei got tired of watching him slide into oblivion, so she kicked Asuka out of the pool to give him some company.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Sun Apr 15, 2012 1:22 am you know what this series needs? more genesis allusions amirite on the whole, that line of thought would be more convincing to me if Rei still possessed agency and wasn't reduced to the impotent audience-substitute by the time OMF takes place. as it is the only two agents in the scene are Shinji and Asuka. doesn't seem profitable to consider external explanations for their presence.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Apr 15, 2012 3:04 am Azathoth wrote: on the whole, that line of thought would be more convincing to me if Rei still possessed agency and wasn't reduced to the impotent audience-substitute by the time OMF takes place.

Some people, from what I've seen, think that it was Shinji's decision, but that doesn't make sense to me because it isn't implied, let alon
